621→    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
622→        node: ASTNode,
623→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
624→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
625→    ) {
626→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
627→            node
628→                .getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody()
629→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
630→                ?: return
631→
632→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
633→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
634→        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
635→
636→        functionBodyBlock
637→            .joinTextToString()
638→            .split("\n")
639→            .firstOrNull()
640→            ?.also {
641→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
642→                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
643→                    if (autoCorrect) {
644→                        functionBodyBlock
645→                            .first()
646→                            .prevLeaf(true)
647→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
648→                    }
649→                }
650→            }
651→    }

674→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
675→            listOf(this)

678→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
679→                .toList()

682→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
683→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
684→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
685→    ): List<ASTNode> {
686→        val iterator = iterator()
687→        var currentNode: ASTNode
688→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
689→
690→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
691→            currentNode = iterator.next()
692→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
693→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
694→                break
695→            }
696→        }
697→
698→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
699→            currentNode = iterator.next()
700→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
701→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
702→                break
703→            }
704→        }
705→
706→        return childrenBetween
707→    }

710→        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }

 36→public class SpacingAroundCurlyRule : StandardRule("curly-spacing") {
 37→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 38→        node: ASTNode,
 39→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 40→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 41→    ) {
 42→        if (node.isLeaf() && !node.isPartOfString()) {
 43→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
 44→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
 45→            val spacingBefore: Boolean
 46→            val spacingAfter: Boolean
 47→            if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
 48→                spacingBefore =
 49→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace ||
 50→                    prevLeaf?.elementType == AT ||
 51→                    (
 52→                        prevLeaf?.elementType == LPAR &&
 53→                            ((node as LeafPsiElement).parent is KtLambdaExpression || node.parent.parent is KtLambdaExpression)
 54→                        )
 55→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || nextLeaf?.elementType == RBRACE
 56→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
 57→                    !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 58→                    prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 59→                        it.elementType == LPAR || it.elementType == AT
 60→                    } == true
 61→                ) {
 62→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 63→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 64→                        prevLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf.node)
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 68→                    (
 69→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let { it.elementType == RPAR || KtTokens.KEYWORDS.contains(it.elementType) } == true ||
 70→                            node.treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 71→                            (prevLeaf.treeParent.elementType == FUN && prevLeaf.treeNext.elementType != LAMBDA_EXPRESSION)
 72→                        ) // allow newline for lambda return type
 73→                ) {
 74→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 75→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                        val eolCommentExists =
 77→                            prevLeaf
 78→                                .prevLeaf()
 79→                                ?.let { it is PsiComment && it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
 80→                                ?: false
 81→                        if (eolCommentExists) {
 82→                            val commentLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()!!
 83→                            if (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
 84→                                (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() as LeafPsiElement).rawRemove()
 85→                            }
 86→                            (node.treeParent.treeParent as TreeElement).removeChild(commentLeaf)
 87→                            (node.treeParent as TreeElement).addChild(commentLeaf, node.treeNext)
 88→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 89→                        }
 90→                        (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
 91→                    }
 92→                }
 93→            } else if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
 94→                spacingBefore = prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || prevLeaf?.elementType == LBRACE
 95→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf == null || nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf)
 96→                if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 97→                    shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf.nextLeaf())
 98→                ) {
 99→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space after \"${node.text}\"", true)
100→                    if (autoCorrect) {
101→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
102→                    }
103→                }
104→            } else {
105→                return
106→            }
107→            when {
108→                !spacingBefore && !spacingAfter -> {
109→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
110→                    if (autoCorrect) {
111→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
112→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
113→                    }
114→                }
115→                !spacingBefore -> {
116→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
117→                    if (autoCorrect) {
118→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
119→                    }
120→                }
121→                !spacingAfter -> {
122→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
123→                    if (autoCorrect) {
124→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
125→                    }
126→                }
127→            }
128→        }
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(leaf: ASTNode?): Boolean {
132→        val nextElementType = leaf?.elementType
133→        return (
134→            nextElementType == DOT ||
135→                nextElementType == COMMA ||
136→                nextElementType == RBRACKET ||
137→                nextElementType == RPAR ||
138→                nextElementType == SEMICOLON ||
139→                nextElementType == SAFE_ACCESS ||
140→                nextElementType == EXCLEXCL ||
141→                nextElementType == LBRACKET ||
142→                nextElementType == LPAR ||
143→                nextElementType == COLONCOLON
144→            )
145→    }
146→}

637→    private fun toEnumClassSequence(fileASTNode: FileASTNode) =

641→            .orEmpty()

621→    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
622→        node: ASTNode,
623→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
624→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
625→    ) {
626→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
627→            node
628→                .getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody()
629→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
630→                ?: return
631→
632→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
633→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
634→        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
635→
636→        functionBodyBlock
637→            .joinTextToString()
638→            .split("\n")
639→            .firstOrNull()
640→            ?.also {
641→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
642→                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
643→                    if (autoCorrect) {
644→                        functionBodyBlock
645→                            .first()
646→                            .prevLeaf(true)
647→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
648→                    }
649→                }
650→            }
651→    }

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→### Removed
 10→
 11→### Fixed
 12→
 13→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 14→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 15→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115) 
 16→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 17→
 18→### Changed
 19→
 20→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 21→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 22→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 23→
 24→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 25→
 26→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 27→
 28→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 29→
 30→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 31→
 32→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 33→
 34→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 35→
 36→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 37→
 38→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 39→
 40→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 41→
 42→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 43→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 44→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 45→
 46→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 47→
 48→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 49→
 50→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 51→
 52→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 53→
 54→### Added
 55→
 56→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 57→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 58→* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 59→* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 60→* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 61→* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 62→* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 63→* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 64→
 65→### Removed
 66→
 67→* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 68→
 69→### Fixed
 70→
 71→* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 72→* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 73→* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 74→* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 75→* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 76→* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 77→
 78→### Changed
 79→
 80→* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 81→* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 82→* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 83→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 84→* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 85→* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 86→* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 87→
 88→## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 89→
 90→### Added
 91→
 92→### Removed
 93→
 94→### Fixed
 95→* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 96→* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 97→* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 98→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 99→* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
100→* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
